title: MPRA RNA-seq and DNA-seq library preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7l4h8","text":"This protocol is for generating MPRA RNA-seq and DNA-seq libraries for sequencing. This protocol uses a transfectable MPRA backbone: Donor_eGP2AP_RC (Addgene: ID#133784). For more detail, see methods section of: https://www.cell.com/cell/fulltext/S0092-8674(24)01435-1","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":212,"length":56}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"5DAA8E0C55DC11F0BCF20A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.cell.com/cell/fulltext/S0092-8674(24)01435-1"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2n414","text":"Extract RNA and DNA from your cells that have been transfected with your MPRA library.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: RNA and DNA extraction: DNA and RNA were extracted from the cells by Quick-DNA/RNAMiniprep Plus Kit (Zymo Research, cat. no. D7003) using 600 uL of shield bufferand 600 uL of lysis buffer per well. RNA and DNA eluted with water at ~55°C
Run reverse transcription (RT) to create cDNA from RNA
Run RT rxn per sample: Use up to 5ug (5ug=5000ng; weight max) or 11uL (volume max) of RNA.
For each RNA sample, make the following mastermix: %s %s Heat for 65 °C for 5 min, ice for 1 min Add the following mixture to finish RT. %s %s 55°C for 1 hour, 80°C for 10 min.
Generate qPCR standards from cDNA to determine transfection efficiency.

Run the following PCR for one of the samples. %s %s %s %s Extract the band at 205 bp using gel extraction (1.8% agarose gel, Zymo Gel DNA Recovery Kit, Cat#D4008) Measure the concentration using Qubit. Perform serial dilution, such that you have 1ng, 100pg, 10pg, 1pg, 100fg, and 10fg/uL stocks. These will serve as standards. *Once standards are made, this step does not need to be repeated for each batch
Use qPCR to estimate the concentration of the MPRA RNA barcodes.
Master mix for cDNA and standards: %s %s %s %s Master mix for DNA samples: %s %s %s %s Run the qPCR with all of these together on the same plate.
qPCR Data Analysis: Example of Data Analysis:

Make a plot of average CT (y axis) and log concentration (x axis) of your standard values. For the x axis log concentration: 100pg would be 5, 10pg would be 4, 1pg would be 3, 100fg would be 2, 10fg would be 1. Get the line equation and R squared value.
For each of your samples, get the average CT value between the two replicates.

Solve your standards’ line equation for X (log concentration). Plug in the average sample CT value in the Y (average CT value), solve for X. Retrieve the concentration (in fg) from X as 10^X. Generally, we are targeting for >700fg/replicate. If your sample has a high transfection efficiency, continue to the next step. If the sample has low transfection efficiency, go back to optimizing transfection efficiency.
Amplify your RNA and DNA library.
Repeat the RT (step 2) for the rest of your RNA samples. Convert all of your RNA to cDNA.

PCR 1 RNA (cDNA) :For RNA samples, use 5uL of your cDNA per 1 rxn. Generally, you will do 18 rxns per sample. Note that this uses Lib_Hand primer. %s %s Temp Time Cycles 1 98 30 sec 1 2 98 10 sec X 9 cycles 3 63 15 sec 4 72 25 sec 5 72 5 min 1 6 4 forever 1 PCR clean up: Split each sample to 2 of the -25 zymo clean and concentrator columns, elute each in 25uL water--> 50uL total per sample
PCR 1 for DNA: This one uses primer Lib_Hand_RT. You will do 4X per one sample. %s %s %s %s PCR clean up: 1 sample use 1 -25 zymo clean and concentrator, elute each in 25uL water

The purpose of qPCR 2 is to estimate the number of PCR cycles for PCR 2. PCR 2 is used to add sequencing adapters and sample indexes. qPCR 2 is optional (you can apply this step only when the samples are overamplified with a certain cycle). Do two reactions for each sample. Pick an index primer at random for qPCR2 and use it for all the samples.
qPCR 2 Master mix: %s %s %s %s Use ¼ of the multicomponent plot (1/4 of the fluorescence from the top and bottom of your lines), follow down this ¼ line to the number of PCR cycles to use.

PCR 2 adds Illumina sequencing adapters and sample indexes. Assign indexes to each sample (each RNA (cDNA) and DNA library should get a unique index). Use all of the product you get from the cleanup steps of PCR 1. DNA samples should have 1.5 rxns per sample. RNA should have 3 rxns per sample. %s %s %s %s The PCR product is cleaned up and size-selected using AMPure XP Beads (Beckman Coulter, cat. no. A63881) with 0.7X and 0.9X ratios to select for a DNA fragment at 273 bp.

The RNA and DNA library samples are pooled together in a 2:1 concentration ratio. Use NovaSeq SP with custom cycles of 35 x 8 x 0 and 20% PhiX and custom sequencing primers. Sequencing Primers:Index primer: TCGGCAGTTGGGAAGAGCATAGTCGTAGAGCACGC R1 primer: CCAAGAAGGGCGGCAAGATCGCCGTGTAATAATTCTAGA
Primers: %s %s P7_ind_#_Han Index primers: %s %s
creator: Jessica McAfee
